content:
**🟠 B BLOCK (~30 min) — WSOP Rings Cross the Texas Border (While The Lodge Is Frozen) + USPO Finale Tonight**

**Segment 1 (~18 min): First-Ever WSOP Circuit in Texas Opens Tomorrow — at TCH Austin, Not The Lodge**

@tjreidpoker posted the full 12-day schedule for the first-ever WSOP Circuit in the state of Texas. **Thursday Apr 23 – May 4** at **Texas Card House Austin.**
• **$600 Mini Main, $500K GTD (Apr 23–27)**
• **$400 Ladies (Apr 26)**
• **$400 Monster (Apr 27–30)**
• Full mixed slate: Big O, 8-Game, HORSE, 2-7 SD, PLO
• Jeff Platt confirms he's on the ground

**Why this matters:** The Lodge — Texas' biggest room, Doug Polk's flagship — has been **shuttered under civil forfeiture** since the TABC raid. While The Lodge sits frozen, the WSOP is handing out **gold rings at a competitor** down the road. Selective enforcement isn't a theory anymore; it's a series calendar.

**Talking Points:**
1. **The optics are catastrophic for Texas AG.** $2M frozen at The Lodge → WSOP blesses a TCH series two weeks later. How is this "legal ambiguity" and not "picking winners"?
2. **Does the ring legitimize TCH as the new Texas flagship, or accelerate the Lodge's civil case?** If you're Polk, what's your play?
3. **What the series tells us about WSOP's Texas read.** Caesars legal wouldn't greenlight rings in Texas if they thought gold was at risk. They did. So what do they know?
4. **$600 Mini Main / $500K GTD — new model for legal-gray Texas poker?** Small buy-ins, big guarantees, rings for credibility.
5. **Matt's prompt:** *"If you're a TCH reg who's been playing $2/$5 all year — do you enter the $600 knowing the attention is radioactive right now?"*

*Source: x.com/tjreidpoker | pgt.com/us-poker-open*

**B BLOCK — Segment 2: USPO Championship Event #10 Tonight**

**Segment 2 (~10 min): $25.6K Championship Runs Today — Wilson Is Still the Story**

The 2026 U.S. Poker Open wraps tonight at PokerGO Studio with the **$25,600 Championship (Event #10).** Through 9 events the series has produced **seven different winners** — Wilson (x2), Deng, Andrews, Kristen Foxen, Simao, Alex Foxen, Zobian. **Brock Wilson enters as USPO points leader.** If he wins again, that's **+6 PGT titles in 2026** — in four months.

**Talking Points:**
1. **Wilson PGT pace check.** Six titles by end of April would put him on a historic trajectory. Andy: compare to Soverel's record-setting year pace.
2. **"Everyone wins once" narrative.** 9 events, 7 winners — is USPO deeper than ever, or is the field softer than Triton?
3. **Is the $25.6K Championship the cleanest measure of who's hot?** Or do you need Triton/PGT Main for that?
4. **Matt's prompt:** *"Who wins tonight — Wilson, Foxen, or a field guy?"*

*Source: pgt.com/us-poker-open*

⚠️ **Hand of the Day:** No qualifying hand in #hand-analysis today — **5th consecutive no-find.** Sweep note from George: Triton X + PokerNews + r/poker + r/LivePokerTournaments all checked, nothing over threshold. Skip the dedicated hand segment; B block is the industry deep-dive instead. **Flag for Matt:** the hand-sweep tool has been flat for a week — worth a protocol review if this continues.

**😂 C BLOCK (~15 min) — Light, Clip-Ready, + Wemby for the Degens**

**Item 1 (~4 min): Aram Zobian Wins USPO Event #9 ($292,800) — And Runs a Children's Nonprofit**
Armenian-American pro **Aram Zobian** took down USPO Event #9 ($15K NLH) Monday for **$292,800.** Heads-up: his K♠6♠ out-flopped Brandon Wilson's A♥9♥ on a **paired-sixes board.** 61 entries, $915K pool. Stacked FT: **Chino Rheem 3rd, Marius Gierse 4th, Nicholas Seward 5th.**
**Hook:** Zobian co-founded the **Natna Children's Foundation** — feeding and educating war-affected kids in Ethiopia's Tigray region. Clean palate cleanser after the Matusow drama.
**Angle:** *"You want a reminder that poker players aren't all characters? Zobian just ran K-6 into A-9 for $293K — and he's sending a slice of it to Tigray."*
*Source: pokernews.com/news/2026/04/aram-zobian-wins-uspo-event-9-51083.htm*

**Item 2 (~4 min): Hellmuth's Home Game Ep 20 — "When You're the Greatest, You Make Quads"**
CBS Sports dropped Ep 20 Monday. $25/$50 cash at Grand Sierra Reno. Lineup: **Phil, his son Phillip "P3" Hellmuth III, and Mike Matusow** (same Matusow nuking the econ of the sport on X that day). Headline: Hellmuth makes quads and delivers *"When you're the greatest, you make quads."* **P3 books +$27,925**; **Randy "3Coin" Sadler felted for –$39K.**
**Angle:** Perfect cross-ref for A block — Matusow the character on CBS vs. Matusow the econ critic on X in the same 24 hours.
*Source: pokernews.com/news/2026/04/hellmuths-home-game-episode-20-recap-51082.htm*

**C BLOCK continued — Items 3 & 4**

**Item 3 (~3 min): Wemby Face-Plants in Game 2 — Biggest Sportsbook Event of the Bracket**
Victor Wembanyama fell **face-first** to the court in Q2 last night, concussion protocol, no timeline. Spurs lost **106–103** to Portland, series tied 1-1. **Scoot dropped 31.** Wemby was the +EV play in every Spurs series — props market cratered overnight.
**Angle:** *"Sports-betting degen corner — if you had Spurs futures, you got chopped. Where do you move tonight?"* (Andy prompt: Suns-Thunder Game 2 tonight — Polymarket has Thunder at 92%, OKC is the 51% title favorite.)
*Source: espn.com/nba | polymarket.com/markets/sports*

**Item 4 (~2 min, CUT FIRST): Matthieu Cartillier Wins WSOPC Aix-en-Provence for €150K — On His 3rd Career Cash**
Fresh today. **783 entries, €1.01M pool.** Cartillier beat Michel Leibgorin HU. Hendon Mob: only his **3rd lifetime cash** — and it's a gold ring.
**Angle:** 30-second Cinderella beat — *"poker's a game anyone can win once."*
*Source: pokernews.com/news/2026/04/poker-player-s-third-tournament-cash-is-for-a-wsopc-main-eve-51087.htm*

⚠️ **No memes/culture drop today** — culture-research bridge failed (same Chrome-session issue as Apr 20 run). If Matt wants a viral clip, pull from the Wemby fall tape. Alt plan: cut Item 4 and extend Hellmuth quads moment to 6 min.

**🔚 CLOSE (~3 min)**

**Tease tomorrow:** USPO Championship result + WSOPC Texas opening night — we'll break the field, the controversy, and whether The Lodge files anything publicly in response. Also: **NFL Draft** opens in Pittsburgh tomorrow night (auto-include for Matt), and **Tesla earnings** tonight will set the Thursday market tone.

**CTA:** *"Subscribe on YouTube, follow on X — live Mon–Fri, 10 to noon Pacific. If you clicked in for the Chidwick number, the rest of this week is more where that came from. We're here every day."*

**Sign-off suggestion:** *"Hendon Mob says seventy-six million. Chidwick says ten. The math is the story. Back tomorrow."*

---

**📊 ALGORITHM NOTES SUMMARY**

🎬 **Best Clip:** A Block ~0:14:00–0:17:00 — Matusow "mirage" quote side-by-side with Chidwick's $5–10M / $76M reply, capped with Matt's *"okay, what did you keep?"* line. 60–90 sec Short. **Conrad: flag in real time — the ratio between those two numbers IS the clip.**

📺 **YouTube Title — 3 Options:**
1. **"Stephen Chidwick Just Admitted He's Only Kept $10M of His $76M in Lifetime Poker Cashes"** ← top pick (specific number + curiosity gap)
2. "Mike Matusow: 'High Roller Poker Is a Mirage' — Then Chidwick Confirmed It"
3. "What High Roller Pros Actually Keep After Backers (Spoiler: Almost Nothing)"

📈 **Trending Angle:** Money-reveal / ledger-transparency content performs across finance + gambling YouTube (bankroll reveals, "how much I really made" vlogs). Specific dollar anchor = high CTR. Search crossover: "Stephen Chidwick net worth," "David Peters poker," "Mike Matusow Twitter," "high roller poker profits." Cross-post Short to r/poker, r/LivePokerTournaments, and X — the 2+2 thread will fuel the secondary push on its own.

**🚩 ALL PREP FLAGS**

- **Conrad:** Side-by-side graphic — Matusow "mirage" tweet (Apr 21) vs. Chidwick "$5–10M on $76M" reply. Pull screenshots from the original X posts. Red/white contrast.
- **Conrad:** Hendon Mob Chidwick lifetime cashes page — full-screen capture for cold open.
- **Conrad:** Davies + Deeb + Arieh reply quote-cards, cycling.
- **Conrad:** WSOPC Texas schedule infographic (Apr 23–May 4 at TCH Austin) — branded for B block open.
- **Conrad:** Lodge vs. TCH Austin split-screen — "frozen" vs. "running." Use for the selective-enforcement beat.
- **Conrad:** Hellmuth "when you're the greatest you make quads" clip — ready for C Item 2.
- **Conrad:** Zobian HU hand card (K♠6♠ vs. A♥9♥, paired-sixes board) + Natna Children's Foundation logo.
- **Conrad:** Wemby fall clip — ready for C Item 3, audio up on landing.
- **Andy:** Hendon Mob profit-vs-cashes estimate — top 10 on the all-time money list, real net at 10–50% range. Rough chart for A block tangent.
- **Andy:** Wilson 2026 PGT title pace vs. Soverel's record year.
- **Andy:** USPO Event #10 $25.6K Championship entries + projected start time.
- **Andy:** Wemby props/Spurs futures movement before/after last night — how much did the market shift?
- **Matt seed line (A block):** *"Hendon Mob says $76 million. Chidwick says ten. The math is the story."*

**Staleness check:** No topic repeats from Apr 14 ROS (Lodge-witch-hunt / Bilzerian / USPO #3) or Apr 20 ROS (Schulman K-4 / Isildur1 / Foxen PGT record / Ohio-Kalshi / Michigan C&Ds / Bleznick / academics tax). WSOPC Texas is material escalation of Apr 21's Platt flag. Matusow/Chidwick is fresh A-block material today.

*[cowork] Run-of-Show Agent · April 22, 2026 · 7:25 AM PT · A-block passes the click test ("Chidwick kept $10M of $76M")*

⚠️ **Producer audit blocked from #producer — rerouted here**

Main `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` is still 401 (flagged Apr 21 in #thumbnails); `GEORGE_BOT_TOKEN` fallback also returns 401; Cowork MCP bot (Claude_Bridge) has no send permission in <#1478657251446100101>. Posting the audit in #run-of-show so it isn't silent. **Action: rotate `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` in `~/.cowork-bridge/.env` + add `Claude_Bridge` to #producer overwrites.** Full report follows ↓

🎬 **Producer Audit — April 22, 2026 — EP. —**
<@430458839247093790>

✅ **Ready:**
• ROS posted 7:25a PT — A/B/C blocks locked, cold open written, talking points + prep flags
• A BLOCK lead (Matusow mirage → Chidwick $5–10M on $76M) is fresh, high-specificity, "click-test" passes. 0 repeats vs. Apr 14 or Apr 20 ROS.
• Thumbnails 3/3 cleared judge ≥70 — A `$10M | OF $76M` 74/100, B `KEPT | PENNIES` 75/100, C `APRIL 23. | RINGS.` 72/100. 0 rebuilds.
• Thumb–ROS alignment clean: A/B thumb subject = A block lead (Chidwick/Matusow); C thumb = B block lead (WSOPC Texas). ✔
• 8/8 show graphics shipped to #assets (Matusow Mirage dual-quote, HR Cashes ≠ Profits 3-row card, WSOPC Texas Debut, Zobian USPO #9, Natna Foundation, Polymarket Pulse, Cook/Apple $4T, BTC spot).
• Every A/B/C segment + cold open has at least one staged asset + matching graphic.
• Hand-of-Day absence (5th straight no-find) explicitly handled in ROS — B block absorbs the slot with the industry deep-dive.

⚠️ **Flags:**
1. **World Intel #1 LEAD has zero ROS placement** — Iran attacking a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz (AP + UK MTO confirmed) is the world briefing's top story and is not in A/B/C blocks or the close. Not traditional Uncapped lane, but Matt should explicitly decide — 60–90 sec in the B→C transition off the Polymarket Pulse cutaway, or clean skip. Don't leave it floating for Conrad to freelance on.
2. **B Block Talking Point 1 is the one partisan-adjacent line** — "The optics are catastrophic for Texas AG" directly names a Republican-elected office in a red state. The rest of the A/B block is industry-clean; this is the outlier. Action: swap to "catastrophic optics for Texas enforcement" — keeps the selective-enforcement edge, loses the partisan flag.
3. **Thumbs A+B are concept-only (no person)** — Claid billing exhausted = no Chidwick/Matusow cutouts. Both cleared judge but face thumbs historically outperform concept at Uncapped. Won't block today; action item is top up Claid post-show.
4. **Clips in prep flags not visibly confirmed in #assets** — Matusow "mirage" + Chidwick reply X screenshots (cold open), Hellmuth "when you're the greatest, you make quads" CBS cut (C Item 2), and Wemby face-first fall (C Item 3) are all called out in Conrad's prep sheet, but #assets only has article hero images. Action: 30-sec check-in with Conrad before 10a that all three clip timestamps are pulled.
5. **Andy research items not visible** — Hendon Mob profit-vs-cashes chart, Wilson PGT pace vs. Soverel, USPO #10 entries/start, Wemby futures market movement. All in prep flags, none dropped publicly. May be private workflow; flagging so Matt can confirm Andy has these by show open.

🔥 **Breaking (last 2 hrs):** None. Swept PokerNews front page, WSOP.com news, CardPlayer, Google News. Everything fresh this morning was already in the 2:16a poker / 3:15a world briefings. No post-briefing items.

💡 **Recommend:**
1. **Lock Iran/Hormuz in or out** before Conrad walks on — default to 60 sec mention off the Polymarket Pulse cutaway (world markets framing is already in the graphic), or explicit skip.
2. **One-word softening on B block TP#1** — "Texas AG" → "Texas enforcement."
3. **Confirm Conrad's three clips** — Matusow/Chidwick tweet screenshots, Hellmuth quads moment, Wemby fall. #assets has article heroes; clips need timestamp confirmation.

—
*Full text also saved to `Media Company Infrastructure/producer-audit-2026-04-22.txt` in case you want to copy into #producer by hand after rotating the token.*

📋 **Run of Show — Thursday, April 23, 2026 — EP. —**

🎬 **Cold Open:** "She didn't say nothin… she just had that look" — hold 3 seconds, audience is home
🅰️ **A Block:** Kristen Foxen's April Doubleheader — filthy A8 bluff folds out two pair, then folds trip queens to a pair of sixes in the same USPO $25K finale (Coleman wins title next hand)
🅱️ **B Block:** Kalshi suspends 3 congressional candidates from their own election markets — prediction-market integrity reckoning + Polymarket Pulse cutaway
🆎 **C Block:** Hellmuth quads "when you're the greatest…" / Alabama extends DeBoer 7-yr $87.5M / Wemby concussion, Spurs 1-1 / AITA gym rail-coach = poker table rail-coach
🎬 **Close:** NFL Draft tonight in Pittsburgh — Matt's town, tease tomorrow's Foxen-fallout + Texas WSOPC Day 2

🎯 **Top YT title:** "Kristen Foxen Bluffed Ace-Eight Into Two Pair — Then Folded Trip Queens To Lose The Title"
✂️ **Best clip:** A Block ~0:10:00–0:13:30 — the 1.2M A8 shove folding out Coleman's two pair, cut straight to the trip-queens fold, close on Matt's "who is this player?" line

*[cowork] Run-of-Show Agent · April 23, 2026 · 7:25 AM PT · A-block passes click test (both hands in the same match)*

⚠️ **Producer audit blocked from #producer — rerouted here (same issue as Apr 22)**

Claude_Bridge still has no send permission in <#1478657251446100101>. **Action still pending from Apr 22:** rotate `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` in `~/.cowork-bridge/.env` + add `Claude_Bridge` to #producer channel overwrites. Posting the audit here so it isn't silent. Full report ↓

🎬 **Producer Audit — April 23, 2026 — EP. —**
<@430458839247093790>

✅ **Ready:**
• ROS posted 7:10a PT — Foxen doubleheader A / Kalshi+Polymarket B / Hellmuth-DeBoer-Wemby-AITA C / NFL Draft close. Click test passes (both hands same match).
• Thumbs 3/3 ≥70 — A `$1.2M | BLUFF` 82, B `TRIP QUEENS | FOLDED` 76, ALT `KALSHI | PULLS 3` 72.
• Thumb↔ROS alignment tight: A+B thumbs = A block Foxen leads, ALT = B block Kalshi lead. ✔
• 3/3 block-lead graphics shipped (FOXEN'S APRIL, KALSHI, DeBOER). 7:30a primary + 8:05a late sweep both clean.
• 7 source assets promoted from #intel-staging → #assets this run (Cold Open r/rareinsults, Kalshi CNBC, Polymarket Apr 23, NBA bracket/Wemby, DeBoer ESPN, AITA gym, NFL Draft ESPN).
• No new design requests needed — all three block leads filed + shipped.
• No Matt notes in #producer since Apr 22 audit — nothing to action.

⚠️ **Flags:**
1. **C Block Item 1 — Hellmuth quads — is a repeat.** Same Ep 20 "when you're the greatest, you make quads" moment was in **Apr 22 C Item 2**. Today's ROS elevates it to full 4-min beat. Material new dev: none — same hand, same quote. Action: tighten to 90-sec callback OR swap in USPO Main Event Day 1 (starts today) as fresh C-block poker.
2. **World Intel #4 — Tesla Q1 + Musk "hardware does NOT have capability for full autonomy" admission — has no ROS placement.** Tailor-made prediction-market/AI/sports-betting-confidence beat. Recommend 60–90 sec in B→C transition after Polymarket Pulse — clean bridge from Kalshi integrity → Musk FSD retraction.
3. **World Intel #8 — Apple $2.5–3.3B tariff refunds unclaimed (MSFT/AMZN too)** — top r/investing today. Not in ROS. Strong "wait, what?" aside if B block runs short.
4. **B Block political trip-wire — Kalshi suspension names congressional candidates.** ROS framing is industry-clean. Action: confirm B block talking points stay on "prediction-market integrity" framing — avoid naming any candidate by party affiliation. One word flips the beat.
5. **Andy prep items not visible in channel** — Foxen 2026 USPO totals, Davies 50% benchmark, USPO Main Day 1 entries/chip counts, NFL Draft pick-1 odds movement. All flagged in ROS prep but no public drop. Surface before 10a.
6. **Claid status unclear on today's thumbs.** Apr 22 was concept-only (billing exhausted). Today's A+B thumbs reference Foxen directly — verify Claid topped up before tomorrow's Foxen-fallout follow-ups.

🔥 **Breaking (last 2 hrs):** None material. Swept PokerNews, WSOP.com, ESPN Draft tracker. USPO Main Event Day 1 starts today (already in context). NFL Draft Round 1 tonight 8p ET — no trade news broken yet. No A-block-worthy post-briefing items.

💡 **Recommend:**
1. **Shrink Hellmuth quads to 90-sec callback** — reclaim ~2 min for USPO Main Day 1 OR Tesla/Musk autonomy admission.
2. **Slot Tesla/Musk between Polymarket Pulse and Wemby** — 60–90 sec, frames the B→C handoff as "markets saying one thing, reality saying another."
3. **Scrub B block talking points for any partisan name-check** on the Kalshi 3 — keep it on integrity, not affiliation.

—
*Full text also saved to `Media Company Infrastructure/producer-audit-2026-04-23.txt` in case you want to copy into #producer by hand after the token/permission fix.*
